title: Ordered-CTR CatBoost on raw race entities
summary: The dataset has very high-cardinality categorical structure (`Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, `Compound`) that hand-made frequency features only approximate.
rationale: The dataset has very high-cardinality categorical structure (`Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, `Compound`) that hand-made frequency features only approximate. CatBoost’s ordered target statistics and categorical combinations are designed to capture sparse interactions using prior objects only, which is a better fit for lap-level strategy patterns than global encodings.
implementation_hint: Train a leak-safe CatBoost classifier on raw categorical columns plus current numeric features; allow combinations like `Driver×Compound`, `Race×Stint`, `Driver×Race`. Build OOF predictions with strict grouped folds by race session and fit everything fold-locally. Compare against the current engineered-feature baseline, and only try a ranking-style CatBoost variant as a secondary blend, not the main model.
expected_effect: Should recover signal from sparse entity interactions and unseen combinations that the current manual preprocessing likely leaves on the table.
risk: If folds are not strict, ordered categorical statistics will still look great locally and fail on the leaderboard; pairwise ranking variants can also disable some useful categorical handling.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
